stories.title: Mother's Day in Colorado Springs — Your 2026 Guide (May 10)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Mother's Day is May 10 in COS 🌿

Wolf tour, brunch, art class, train ride, free Farmers Market — there's something for every family and every budget.

Our full COS guide is at the link.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
COS families — Mother's Day spots fill fast 🌸

Wolf & Wildlife Center (May 10, 9–11 AM): reservations required.
Painting with a Twist: May 9.
Fine Arts Center brunch: book now.

Full guide at the link.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
Mother's Day is one week away, Colorado Springs families — and there's a lot happening right here in COS. 🌿

Whether you're looking for something free and relaxed or a proper splurge, here's what's on the table for Sunday, May 10:

**If you want to get outdoors:** The Downtown Farmers Market opens its 2026 season that same day at Acacia Park (9 AM–2 PM, free). Pack a tote and make it a family morning.

**If you want wildlife:** The Colorado Wolf & Wildlife Center is offering wolf tours on May 10 from 9–11 AM. Tickets are $40 for adults and $20 for kids ages 6–11. Reservations are required — grab them while they're available.

**If you want brunch:** Lumen8 Rooftop Social is doing a Mother's Day brunch from 10 AM–2 PM ($70 adults, $35 for kids 6–12, free under 5). The Fine Arts Center at Colorado College has seating from 10 AM–12:45 PM ($115 adults, $25 for kids 12 and under). Both require reservations.

**If you want a train adventure:** The Royal Gorge Route Railroad is running departures May 9–10 with a gourmet dining option or casual fare from the 403 Grill.

**If you want art:** Painting with a Twist (115 N Tejon St) has a special Mother's Day class on Saturday, May 9 — morning or afternoon sessions from $45–$70.

Something for every family this Sunday. What are you planning to do? 🌸
facebook.image_caption: Mother's Day is May 10, COS families 🌿 From wolf tours to farmers market mornings to brunch with mountain views — we've rounded up the best options for a special Sunday. Check the link for the full guide, including where reservations are required.

article.headline: Mother's Day in Colorado Springs 2026: A Family Guide to Events, Brunch, and Free Outdoor Fun

stories.title: CPSC Recall — 227,500 Stephan Baby Plush Toys Pulled for Choking Hazard

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: 227,500 Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie plush toys pulled.

The water-filled cube breaks into sharp pieces — choking and laceration hazard. 1 child swallowed pieces.

Stop using the cube now. See cpsc.gov/Recalls

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Check your shelves: Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie plush toys sold at Amazon, Target, and Walmart (Aug 2017–Jan 2026) are recalled.

No receipt? You still get a $10 refund. See cpsc.gov/Recalls.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
COS parents — please check your home for this product. ⚠️

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has recalled about 227,500 Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends children's plush toys. These are small plush animals that come with a removable, water-filled plastic cube designed to be chilled for comfort.

Here's the problem: that plastic cube can break into smaller, sharp pieces. The CPSC has received 20 reports of the cube breaking apart, including one incident where a child swallowed sharp pieces.

These were sold at Amazon, Target, Walmart, and independent stores across the country from August 2017 through January 2026. If you bought one over the years as a gift or teething comfort item, it may still be in your home.

What to do right now: take the plastic cube away from your child and stop using it. You don't have to throw the whole toy away — just the cube.

For a refund: if you have proof of purchase, you'll receive a full refund. No receipt? You can still get a $10 refund by check or a $20 store credit at stephanbaby.com. Go to cpsc.gov/Recalls for the full details.

Has anyone spotted this toy in their house? Drop a comment so other families know to check too. 👇
facebook.image_caption: ⚠️ RECALL ALERT: About 227,500 Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie plush toys recalled. The removable plastic cube can break into sharp pieces. Remove the cube now and visit cpsc.gov/Recalls for refund info. Sold at Amazon, Target, and Walmart since 2017.

article.headline: CPSC Recall: 227,500 Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie Plush Toys Pulled for Choking and Laceration Hazard

All at Ed Robson Arena, Colorado College. Last day of school: May 22.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
COS District 11 families — graduation season is almost here. 🎓

Here's what you need to know for the Class of 2026 ceremonies:

**Palmer High School:** May 19 at 9:00 AM
**Mitchell High School:** May 19 at 3:00 PM
**Coronado High School:** May 20 at 9:00 AM
**Doherty High School:** May 20 at 3:00 PM
**Achieve, Tesla, Bijou, and Odyssey:** May 21 (times TBD)

All ceremonies are held at Ed Robson Arena at Colorado College (unless otherwise noted by the school).

The last day of school for D11 students is May 22 — just a couple weeks away.

If you're a D11 family, now's the time to sort out tickets, parking, and plans. Do you have a graduate in the house this year? Tell us which school they're graduating from — we're celebrating every single one of you. 🎉

stories.title: AAP Releases New Screen Time Guidelines for 2026 — Less About Hours, More About Habits

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The AAP just dropped the 2-hour screen time rule for kids 6+.

The new 2026 guidelines focus on quality, context, and habits — not hour counts.

Under 2: still no screens. Ages 2–5: 1 hour of good content.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
COS parents — the American Academy of Pediatrics changed the screen time rules, and honestly? We're here for it. 📱

The AAP released updated guidelines in early 2026, and the big headline is this: for kids ages 6 and older, they've dropped the strict "no more than 2 hours per day" rule. Instead, the new approach focuses on quality, context, and habits — not just counting minutes.

Here's what didn't change: under 18 months, screens are still a no. For ages 2–5, the recommendation is still one hour per day of high-quality content.

What's the new framework? The AAP calls it the "5 Cs" — and the goal is to help parents think about whether screen time is adding to or taking away from their child's development, rather than just watching the clock.

The practical takeaways are things most parents already know but maybe needed permission to prioritize: keep bedrooms and mealtimes device-free, co-watch and talk about what they're seeing, and remember that your own habits set the tone.

This doesn't mean screens are suddenly fine in unlimited quantities. It means pediatricians are acknowledging that a 10-year-old doing creative things online for three hours isn't the same as a 10-year-old mindlessly scrolling for three hours.

What do you think of the new approach — relief, or do you prefer having a clear limit? 👇
facebook.image_caption: The AAP dropped the strict 2-hour screen time rule for older kids 📱 The new 2026 guidelines focus on quality, context, and habits through the "5 Cs" framework. Under 18 months: still no screens. Ages 2–5: still 1 hour/day. What do COS families think of the change?

article.headline: AAP Changes Screen Time Guidelines for 2026: What COS Parents Need to Know

stories.title: Garden of the Gods Art Festival — May 16–17 at Rock Ledge Ranch (Free for Kids Under 12)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
May 16–17 at Rock Ledge Ranch: Garden of the Gods Art Festival.

150 artists. Live music. Food trucks. Kids activities. Pikes Peak views.

$10 adults. Free under 12. Free shuttle from Coronado HS.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
If you're looking for a perfect spring family day in COS, we've got one for you: the Garden of the Gods Art Festival is coming up May 16–17 at Rock Ledge Ranch. 🎨

Here's what you need to know:

**When:** May 16–17, 10 AM–5 PM both days
**Where:** Rock Ledge Ranch Historic Site, Colorado Springs
**Admission:** $10 for adults, $8 for seniors 65+ and military, and free for children under 12 (and free for Rock Ledge members)

This is the 3rd annual festival, and it features 150 juried fine artists and craftspeople from across the country — pottery, jewelry, textiles, paintings, sculpture, glass art, and more. There's live music, food trucks, kids activities, and the kind of backdrop (hello, Pikes Peak) that you just can't get anywhere else.

Pro tip: there's a free shuttle running from Coronado High School at 1590 W. Fillmore St. from 9:30 AM–5 PM both days if you'd rather skip the parking hunt.

Admission proceeds benefit Rock Ledge Ranch, so your $10 is doing something good for the community.

Are you planning to go? What kind of art are your kids most excited about? 🖼️
facebook.image_caption: 3rd Annual Garden of the Gods Art Festival: May 16–17 at Rock Ledge Ranch 🎨 150 artists, live music, food trucks, kids activities — and free for kids under 12. Plus a free shuttle from Coronado High School. Full details in the link.

article.headline: Garden of the Gods Art Festival May 16–17: 150 Artists, Free for Kids, and a Pikes Peak Backdrop
